notes: Lesson 25 Part 2 (act 148) 'Rate Limiting — the token bucket and the burst trade-off', 132.2s, 6 cues. Uses sysdesign.py token_bucket/request_mark/timeline/tick. Verified: capacity+refill state, spend-3-tokens, burst-drain-then-reject, lazy min(cap,tok+Δt·rate)=4 refill (no timer), sliding-window rolling count (3 ticks enclosed = count 3), tool-pick token-bucket-vs-sliding-window with O(1) bound. 2 -ql review iterations (round-1 caught sliding-window tick/count mismatch — window now encloses exactly the counted ticks); READ start/mid/end each cue from muxed mp4, all pass.

frames.notes: [start] token bucket capacity 5 (5 emerald tokens stacked), chips capacity=5 / refill=1 token per sec / each request spends 1; note 'the bucket is the only state: a count of tokens plus a timestamp'. Bucket idiom + rate correct.

[mid] token bucket capacity 5 (5 emerald tokens stacked), chips capacity=5 / refill=1 token per sec / each request spends 1; note 'the bucket is the only state: a count of tokens plus a timestamp'. Bucket idiom + rate correct.

[end] token bucket capacity 5 (5 emerald tokens stacked), chips capacity=5 / refill=1 token per sec / each request spends 1; note 'the bucket is the only state: a count of tokens plus a timestamp'. Bucket idiom + rate correct.

[start] three requests spend three tokens: bucket drains 5->2 as req1✓/req2✓/req3✓ fade in green, 'tokens = N' chip transforms down; note 'all three allowed — bucket still had tokens'. Spend-while-available correct.

[mid] three requests spend three tokens: bucket drains 5->2 as req1✓/req2✓/req3✓ fade in green, 'tokens = N' chip transforms down; note 'all three allowed — bucket still had tokens'. Spend-while-available correct.

[end] three requests spend three tokens: bucket drains 5->2 as req1✓/req2✓/req3✓ fade in green, 'tokens = N' chip transforms down; note 'all three allowed — bucket still had tokens'. Spend-while-available correct.

[start] a burst of 5 drains the bucket to zero (tokens=0 rose, empty! label, empty outline), then req6✗/req7✗ rejected; note 'once empty, requests rejected until it refills'. Burst-then-reject correct.
[mid] a burst of 5 drains the bucket to zero (tokens=0 rose, empty! label, empty outline), then req6✗/req7✗ rejected; note 'once empty, requests rejected until it refills'. Burst-then-reject correct.
[end] a burst of 5 drains the bucket to zero (tokens=0 rose, empty! label, empty outline), then req6✗/req7✗ rejected; note 'once empty, requests rejected until it refills'. Burst-then-reject correct.

[start] lazy refill: rule tokens=min(cap, tokens+Δt×rate); '3 sec elapsed, no request'; calc 1+3×1=4 refills bottom-4 tokens (top slot stays empty, capped); 'no background timer — computed on read' note. Lazy min-cap refill math correct.

[mid] lazy refill: rule tokens=min(cap, tokens+Δt×rate); '3 sec elapsed, no request'; calc 1+3×1=4 refills bottom-4 tokens (top slot stays empty, capped); 'no background timer — computed on read' note. Lazy min-cap refill math correct.

[end] lazy refill: rule tokens=min(cap, tokens+Δt×rate); '3 sec elapsed, no request'; calc 1+3×1=4 refills bottom-4 tokens (top slot stays empty, capped); 'no background timer — computed on read' note. Lazy min-cap refill math correct.

[start] sliding-window counter: time axis with 5 ticks, blue window 'last 60s' enclosing exactly 3 ticks; 'count = 3 ≤ limit 4 → allow' emerald; note 'no bursts: counts timestamps in window and blocks over the limit'. Window count matches enclosed ticks — correct.

[mid] sliding-window counter: time axis with 5 ticks, blue window 'last 60s' enclosing exactly 3 ticks; 'count = 3 ≤ limit 4 → allow' emerald; note 'no bursts: counts timestamps in window and blocks over the limit'. Window count matches enclosed ticks — correct.

[end] sliding-window counter: time axis with 5 ticks, blue window 'last 60s' enclosing exactly 3 ticks; 'count = 3 ≤ limit 4 → allow' emerald; note 'no bursts: counts timestamps in window and blocks over the limit'. Window count matches enclosed ticks — correct.

[start] pick the tool: token bucket (emerald, friendly bursts to capacity + average rate) vs sliding window (blue, strict no-burst rolling count) split by a divider; O(1) time, O(1) state per client bound. Trade-off + complexity correct.

[mid] pick the tool: token bucket (emerald, friendly bursts to capacity + average rate) vs sliding window (blue, strict no-burst rolling count) split by a divider; O(1) time, O(1) state per client bound. Trade-off + complexity correct.

[end] pick the tool: token bucket (emerald, friendly bursts to capacity + average rate) vs sliding window (blue, strict no-burst rolling count) split by a divider; O(1) time, O(1) state per client bound. Trade-off + complexity correct.
